concept: Compose / To Ground a Whole Connection
description: The act of arranging artistic or linguistic elements into a structured, harmonious whole. It is a deliberate act of creation that brings order and connection to disparate parts.
rationale: Composed from the established roots 'tho' (a whole system), 'ma' (grounded presence), and 'lo' (connection). The word's structure literally means 'to ground a whole connection.' This perfectly captures the act of composing as a deliberate, structural process of creating a unified and grounded piece of work.
